exercises.name: SB toe rolls
exercises.description: Slowly roll the SB up with your finger tips to your toes and then slowly roll it back down. The entire time core should be tight and engaged with “hold your pee” contraction.

exercises.name: SB W
exercises.description: You can do this exercise with no weight or very light weights. Squeeze shoulder blades and keep shoulders low. Do not shrug

exercises.name: SB wide DB row
exercises.description: Keep arms out wide, pull back and squeeze shoulder blades.

exercises.name: Scapular Clock Exercise
exercises.description: Scapular Clock Exercise

exercises.name: Scapular Pull Up
exercises.description:
1. Start by hanging from a pull up bar, with hands roughly shoulder-width apart. 

2. Bring your shoulder blades downwards and together, by bringing the shoulders away from the ears.

3. Avoid bending at the elbows. Slowly return to a hanging position, and repeat.

exercises.name: Scapular Pushups
exercises.description:
1. Start in a plank position on your hands and toes. 

2. Allow your torso to drop slightly - bringing your shoulder blades together. 

3. Push the floor away, spreading your shoulder blades away from each other as far as you can.

4. Slowly return to the start position.

exercises.name: Scapular Pushups from Elbows
exercises.description:
1. Start in a plank position on your elbows. 

2. Allow your torso to drop slightly - bringing your shoulder blades together. 

3. Push the floor away, spreading your shoulder blades away from each other as far as you can.

exercises.name: Schwinn Airdyne Pro vs Rogue Echo Bike - Side by Side Comparison
exercises.description: Schwinn Airdyne Pro vs Rogue Echo Bike - Side by Side Comparison

exercises.name: Sciatic Nerve Glide
exercises.description:
1. Start by lying on your back with one foot flat on the floor, and the opposite knee in towards the chest.

2. Wrap your hands in behind the thigh of the top leg and gently draw that leg in towards your body.

3. Slowly extend the top leg, reaching the toes towards the ceiling, then lowering back down to the start position. 

4. Ensure you're only feeling a gentle stretch, and no burning or tingling sensations.

5. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Seal Jack to Squat
exercises.description:
Start standing with your feet together and arms extended in front of you at shoulder height. Jump your feet outward while simultaneously opening your arms wide to the sides, mimicking a “seal jack” motion. 

Land softly and immediately lower into a squat, keeping your chest up and knees tracking over your toes. Press through your heels to stand back up and return to the starting position. 

Repeat continuously, flowing smoothly between the seal jack and squat.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Curl to Press
exercises.description:
Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward, and elbows close to your torso. 

Perform a bicep curl by bending your elbows and bringing the dumbbells toward your shoulders. Once at the top of the curl, rotate your wrists so palms face forward and press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended. 

Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height, then slowly return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Front Raise to Lateral Raise
exercises.alternateName: Seated Dumbbell Front Raise to side Raise
exercises.description:
Sit on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your thighs. Begin by lifting the dumbbells straight in front of you to shoulder height (front raise) with a slight bend in your elbows. 

Lower the dumbbells back down slowly, then immediately lift them out to your sides to shoulder height (lateral raise), keeping a slight bend in your elbows. 

Lower the dumbbells back down to your thighs and repeat the full sequence for reps.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Hammer Curl to Neutral Press
exercises.description:
Sit on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing each other (neutral grip). 

Perform a hammer curl by bending your elbows and bringing the dumbbells toward your shoulders. From the top of the curl, press the dumbbells straight up overhead, keeping palms facing each other, until your arms are fully extended. 

Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height, then slowly return to the starting position. Repeat for reps.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Row to Rear Delt Fly
exercises.description:
Sit on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing your torso. Hinge slightly at the hips while keeping your back straight. 

Perform a row by pulling the dumbbells toward your torso, squeezing your shoulder blades together. 

Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position. From here, hinge forward slightly and lift the dumbbells out to your sides in a rear delt fly motion, keeping a slight bend in your elbows. 

Lower the dumbbells back down and repeat the full sequence for reps.

exercises.name: Seated Hip Twist
exercises.alternateName: windshield wipers, knee drops, hip floss
exercises.description:
1) Start from a seated position feet together, knees bent, hands on the floor behind you.

2) Rotate the hip by bringing your knees to the floor on one side.

3) Then rotate to the other side.

4) Repeat and alternate.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Press
exercises.description:
1. Sit on a leg press machine, and place your feet against the pad about shoulder-width apart. Make sure your knees are bent at the start of the exercise. 

2. Slowly push against the pad using your heels until your knees are almost straight.  Do not lock your knees straight at the peak of this exercise. Then move back into the start position.

3. Repeat for each rep.

exercises.name: Seated Machine Ab Crunch
exercises.description:
1. Sit at an Ab Crunch machine and follow the directions on the machine for the correct setup position. 

2. Bring your ribs down towards your pelvis by squeezing your abs.

3. Return to the start position and repeat.

exercises.name: Seated Neutral Grip DB Shoulder Press
exercises.description: Seated Neutral Grip DB Shoulder Press

exercises.name: Seated Piriformis Stretch
exercises.description:
1.For this stretch, cross your legs in a seated position. Sit up tall through your torso.

2.Apply light pressure downward through the knee.

3.Feel the stretch in the front part of your hip allowing it to open up as you apply the light pressure.

4. Once you're done holding, switch and do the other side.

exercises.name: Seated Reverse Fly
exercises.description:
1) While performing the reverse fly, imagine that you are trying to squeeze a grape between your shoulder blades. 

2) Keep your arms mostly straight with a soft elbow bend. 

3) Then forward.

exercises.name: Seated Single Arm Chest Press
exercises.description:
1.Sit upright making sure back is rested on the backrest, one holding the horizontal position handle and close to your shoulder, elbow bent.

2.Push the handle forward to a straight arm position in front of your chest.

3.Bending at the elbow, slowly return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Shoulder Tap
exercises.description:
1) When this exercise, maintain a straight line throughout your body from head to feet. 

2) Get in a plank position, keep your glutes on. Hands underneath your shoulders and feet roughly shoulder-width apart. 

3) From here, squeeze your glutes and abdominals as you tap up to the other side with the opposite hand. 

4) Maintain your body position without your hips lifting or body rotation.
